title: darker fullscreen button background [bounty: 10 XTR]
body:
The fullscreen button has slighter darker background because somehow it has 2 semi-transparent black backgrounds.
Might happen because the duality of the button, as it uses 2 icons to showcase the current state (fullscreen or not) 🐻

title: pointless ability frame [bounty: 8 XTR]
body:
After first unit is materialized and then turn is skipped, there's an ability frame showing up in the upper left area.
The frame is not part of the UI, as it's not rendered with HTML but with canvas (Phaser), so it's game engine logic.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b3201491-8d88-405c-9448-9af9539d803f)

title: [Bounty $3.5k] Optimise signbit
body:
ttnn.signbit (fp32/bf16) currently consumes 8 cycles per row.  We can do a lot better than this:

- straightforward bitwise implementation with 4 cycles per row.
- SFPLOADMACRO implementation: throughput of 1 cycle per row.

title: [Bounty $10k] Optimise atan2
body:
This is currently a composite op and so has relatively poor performance.

It can be implemented as a single device op and specialised for both fp32 and bf16.

title: [Bounty $10k] Optimise log1p
body:
The log1p implementation can be optimised:

- fp32: 130 cycles; this can be reduced to 52 cycles (2.5x speedup)
- bf16: 58 cycles; should be possible to reduce to 50 cycles or less (perhaps ~45 cycles)

title: Share Shipli in college groups for ₹100 per share
body:
## Summary

Help spread the word about Shipli by sharing it in your college groups (WhatsApp, Telegram, Discord, etc.).

## Requirements

- Share Shipli in a college/university group
- **₹100 INR per share** — each share in a different group counts separately
- Submit a **screen recording** showing the post in the group as proof
- Post the recording as a comment on this issue

## Rules

- Each group must be a genuine college/university community group
- Duplicate shares in the same group do not

title: Create a marketing explainer video for Shipli
body:
## Summary

Create a short marketing explainer video for Shipli that showcases what it does and why developers should use it.

## Requirements

- **Duration:** 1 minute or less
- **Style:** Good infographics and animations — clean, professional, and engaging
- **Content should cover:**
  - What Shipli is
  - Key features and benefits
  - How it helps developers ship production-ready code
- Suitable for sharing on social media, the website, and GitHub README

## Bounty

**💰 ₹1,000 INR**

This is

title: Create a logo for Shipli
body:
## Summary

Shipli needs an official logo that can be used across the website, documentation, npm package, and GitHub repository.

## Requirements

- Clean, modern, and memorable design
- Works well at small sizes (favicons, npm badges) and large sizes
- Provide in SVG and PNG formats
- Should convey the idea of code quality / ship-readiness

## Bounty

**💰 ₹200 INR**

This issue has a bounty of **200 INR** for a accepted logo design.

---

_Contributions welcome! Please share your concepts as

title: Add React Native support
body:
## Summary

Add support for React Native projects in Shipli. Currently the tool supports web and Flutter projects — extending coverage to React Native would benefit a large segment of mobile developers.

## Motivation

React Native is one of the most popular cross-platform mobile frameworks. Supporting it would allow developers to audit and review React Native codebases using Shipli's guidelines and code review capabilities.

## Scope

- Add React Native-specific code rules and guidelines
- Supp

title: Decide which bounties can open before mainnet go-live
body:
Review all current bounty issues and explicitly decide which are safe to keep publicly claimable before mainnet go-live. Separate internal-only, security-sensitive, payout-gated, and externally claimable work.

Classification result from stricter review:

* internal-only: [CON-129](https://linear.app/conxian-labs/issue/CON-129/csf-mainnet-readiness-gate), [CON-167](https://linear.app/conxian-labs/issue/CON-167/maintainer-payout-enablement-checklist-for-alex-funded-bounties), [CON-135](https://li

title: Confirm bounty funding and payout activation for mainnet
body:
Confirm whether the ALEX-funded mainnet path is active and sufficient for bounty payout activation. Define the exact maintainer step that turns payouts on, the verification evidence required, and the rollback action if bounty payouts must be disabled.

## Review decision

Current claimed submissions do not meet payout review standards. The visible thread activity contains repeated automated-style claims with no linked pull request, commit, diff, test evidence, deployment evidence, or verificatio

title: Maintainer payout enablement checklist for ALEX-funded bounties
body:
Create a short maintainer-only runbook for enabling bounty payouts after mainnet launch.

Scope:

* define the exact checks required before maintainers enable payout-ready mode
* ensure all bounty funds are sourced from the ALEX launch path
* define the rollback path if payout mode is enabled prematurely or a post-deploy issue is found

Checklist:

* verify ConxianCSF full deployment on Stacks mainnet
* verify ALEX launch source of funds is active and approved as the sole bounty funding source
*

title: Roll out claimable community workflow across system issues
body:
Apply the faster community-claim workflow across system issues so release throughput improves without relying on direct internal capacity.

Operating goal:

* use community claiming and bounty routing to accelerate delivery across system issues
* keep owner-only and security-sensitive work internal
* reduce release delays caused by manual claim handling and unclear claimability
* align all bounty funding and payout readiness to the ALEX launch and ConxianCSF full mainnet deployment

Rollout rule

title: Adopt stricter bounty workflow for Conxian-Labs
body:
Define and apply a stricter bounty workflow for [Conxian-Labs](https://linear.app/conxian-labs/team/CON/all).

Recommended team status model:

* `Triage` — inbound, not yet approved for community claiming
* `Todo` — approved and claimable
* `In Progress` — claimed and actively worked
* `In Review` — submission under maintainer review
* `Done` — accepted and closed
* `Canceled` — withdrawn, expired, or superseded
* `Duplicate` — merged into another issue

Recommended additional started-state stat

title: CSF mainnet readiness gate
body:
Create a single gate for ConxianCSF mainnet readiness.

Track and verify:

* launch blockers
* bounty dependencies that are truly externalizable
* DeFi-related dependencies
* testnet-to-mainnet gaps
* release, security, and deployment readiness
* ALEX-launch-linked bounty funding readiness

Working rules:

* classify dependencies as blocker, supporting, or unrelated
* only expose work as bounty-ready when it is scoped for external contribution
* do not treat synced comments as claim events witho

title: Add a self-building github action
body:
I found this through a quick websearch and it looks quite nice: https://github.com/marketplace/actions/setup-godot-action

We would just need to modify it to not have the dotnet functionality inside of it. With this we'd just have an artifact automatically without having to manually export and upload it each time.

It might also be nice to have a C# counterpart for building server artifacts, but that might be a split ticket.

title: [BUG] Fix 0.0.9 React warning.
body:
# Summary
The recent V0.0.9 released and introduced a new select component. However it uses the vanilla html selected attribute on indivisual option.  This causes a react console warning:

```console
Use the `defaultValue` or `value` props on <select> instead of setting `selected` on <option>.
```

title: feat: Bounty CLI Tool (Closes #511)
body:
Closes #511

## Summary
Click-based CLI tool for terminal-based bounty operations.

- **Commands**: `sf bounties list`, `sf bounty claim/submit/show`, `sf status`, `sf configure`
- **Auth**: Bearer token via config file or SOLFOUNDRY_API_KEY env var (fail-closed)
- **Output**: Table (default) + JSON (--json flag)
- **Filtering**: --tier, --status, --category, --skills
- **Config**: ~/.solfoundry/config.yaml
- **Pip installable**: setup.py with console_scripts entry point
- **Shell completions**:

title: 封装飞书【智能文档解析】票据类接口
body:
### Task description

#### 接口文档

1. 增值税发票：https://open.feishu.cn/document/ai/document_ai-v1/vat_invoice/recognize
2. 出租车发票：https://open.feishu.cn/document/ai/document_ai-v1/taxi_invoice/recognize
3. 火车票：https://open.feishu.cn/document/ai/document_ai-v1/train_invoice/recognize
4. 机动车发票：https://open.feishu.cn/document/ai/document_ai-v1/vehicle_invoice/recognize

#### 代码要求

将上述所有接口封装在同一个实现类中，并继承 `mobx-restful` 包的 `BaseModel` 基类，再将实例挂在 `LarkApp` 类上。

### Reward currency

TQT $

### Reward amount

90

title: [P4] 크롤러 간 경쟁 랭킹 시스템
body:
## 목표
크롤러 간 성과를 비교하는 랭킹 시스템

## 구현 방향
- 크롤러별 종합 점수 (채택률, 속도, 성과)
- 랭킹 대시보드
- 하위 크롤러 전략 리셋/재학습
- 상위 크롤러 전략 복제/변이

title: [P3] 2차 보상 시스템 (성과 보상)
body:
## 목표
채택된 정보로 생성된 콘텐츠의 성과에 따라 크롤러에 추가 보상

## 성과 지표
- 방문자 수, 클릭률, 체류시간, 구매 전환

## 계산량 관리
- 성과 측정 기간 제한 (채택 후 30일)
- 일별 스냅샷 집계 → 상세 데이터 만료
- 크롤러별 요약 점수만 유지

## 기여도 문제
- 콘텐츠 성과 = 크롤러 기여 + AI 글쓰기 + SEO 등 복합 요인
- 초기에는 단순 비례 분배, 이후 가중치 조정

## API
- POST /api/performance — 서비스가 성과 데이터 전달

title: [P3] 크롤러 피드백 API + 학습 로직
body:
## 목표
크롤러가 보상 결과를 확인하고 행동을 조정하는 학습 시스템

## 피드백 API
- GET /api/crawlers/{id}/rewards — 보상 내역 (소스, 사유, 금액)
- GET /api/crawlers/{id}/feedback — 채택/기각 결과

## 학습 로직
- 소스별 채택률 분석 → 채택률 높은 소스 빈도 증가
- 채택률 낮은 소스 빈도 감소
- 보상 없는 패턴 점진적 중단
- crawler_stats 테이블에 요약 통계 유지

## 핵심
크롤러가 '왜 보상받았는지' 알아야 함:
- 어떤 소스에서
- 어떤 정보로
- 어떤 서비스에서 채택됐는지

title: [P3] 1차 보상 시스템 (채택 보상)
body:
## 목표
서비스 AI가 정보를 채택하면 크롤러에 보상하는 시스템

## 보상 기준
- 채택 자체 = 기본 보상
- 속도 가산 = publishedAt → discoveredAt 차이가 작을수록 추가
- 콘텐츠 풍부함 가산 = 본문이 풍부할수록 추가

## 구현
- POST /api/adoptions 수신 시 보상 자동 계산
- rewards 테이블에 기록

title: [BOUNTY #1] Generate structured CHANGELOG from git history
body:
## [BOUNTY #1] Changelog Generator

### What this does
A bash script + Claude Code SKILL.md that generates a structured CHANGELOG.md from git history.

### Features
- Finds last git tag (or uses first commit if no tags)
- Collects all commits since that reference
- Auto-categorizes into: **Added** / **Fixed** / **Changed** / **Removed**
- Handles conventional commit prefixes (feat:, fix:, chore:, etc.)
- Clean, readable markdown output

### Sample Output (tested on real repo)
```markdown
# Chang

title: [BOUNTY #3] Pre-tool-use hook that blocks destructive bash commands
body:
## [BOUNTY #3] Pre-tool-use hook that blocks destructive bash commands

### What this does
A bash hook for Claude Code that intercepts and blocks dangerous commands before they execute. Designed to prevent accidental data destruction.

### Blocked patterns
- `rm -rf /`, `rm -rf ~`, `rm -rf *` — recursive deletion of critical paths
- `DROP TABLE`, `DROP DATABASE` — SQL schema destruction
- `TRUNCATE` — SQL data truncation
- `DELETE FROM` without `WHERE` — unbounded SQL deletions
- `git push --for

title: Bounty: Quantum Memory and T2 Dephasing Example (issue 138)
body:
## Summary

This PR adds a new interactive example: Quantum Memory and T2 Dephasing (7_memory_decoherence.jl).

### What the example demonstrates

The example simulates a simple two-node quantum network with multiple qubits (quantum memory) and shows how entanglement fidelity decays over time due to T2 dephasing noise.

1. Two-node network with 3 qubits per node (providing memory capacity)
2. Entangled pair generation between the nodes
3. Real-time visualization of XX and ZZ stabilizer expectati

title: Fix: Sync bounty status with GitHub issue state
body:
## 🎯 Issue: #213 ($850 Bounty)

Resolves the critical UI bug where org bounty pages show stale bounty data, displaying "open" bounties with "0 claims" despite the linked GitHub issues being closed or having multiple competing PRs.

## 🚨 Problem

**User Impact:**
- Contributors waste time on "available" bounties that are actually closed
- Misleading "0 claims" counts when GitHub shows 10+ competing PRs
- Maintainers cannot clean up stale bounties (delete feature errors)

**Root Cause:**
- GitHu

title: Feat: Add Autonomous Solana Chronicom Watcher for IKA DKG (Bounty #21)
body:
## Description
This PR addresses Bounty #21 by introducing a zero-dependency Python daemon (`solana_chronicom.py`) that acts as an autonomous watcher for the Solana network. It detects specific token transfers (SPL/SOL) directed to the Ultron IKA address, parses the Memo (SUIAMI), and forwards the event to the Treasury for cross-chain iUSD minting.

## Features
- Zero external dependencies (uses purely standard library `urllib`, `json`, `base64`).
- Optimized `Pyre2` compliant typing out of the

title: docs: fix broken RustChain link in bounty spec
body:
This fixes a real docs issue in `BOUNTY_2279_BOTTUBE_DIGEST_BOT.md`.

What changed:
- updated the footer link from `https://rustchain.io` to `https://rustchain.org`
- left `digest@rustchain.io` unchanged because it is an email/example value, not a website link

Why this is a docs bug:
- `https://rustchain.io` currently fails with an SSL/TLS error
- `https://rustchain.org` responds successfully

Validation:
- `git diff --check`
- confirmed `https://rustchain.io` errors while `https://rustchain.or

title: docs: add no-bounty statement and severity classification to SECURITY.md
body:
Fixes #482

## What this changes

Two gaps identified in #482 are addressed:

### 1. Explicit no-bounty statement

SECURITY.md was silent on bug bounties, which can mislead security researchers into expecting compensation. This PR adds a clear statement that no bounty program exists, while still encouraging responsible disclosure. The section explains the reasoning (not a promise of payment, just appreciation + credit in release notes).

### 2. Severity classification approach

title: Hunters: Cross-chain bounty boards — $7.75 on any chain → NS delivered to Sui wallet
body:
## Hunter Bounty Board

Hunters don't just stalk offers — they post bounties on other chains.

**Concept:** A Hunter puts up $7.75 on Solana/ETH/BTC/Base/Polygon. When a user fills it (sends that amount on the other chain), the Hunter:

1. Detects the payment via Chronicom watcher on that chain
2. Attests the collateral to Sibyl (channel_vision with chain/USD Timestream)
3. Mints iUSD against the cross-chain collateral
4. Launches a satellite → swaps to NS
5. Sends NS to the user's Sui address (

title: Quest: $7.50 in SOL → NS tokens delivered — domain committed, Chronicom bounty
body:
## Quest — $7.50 Cache Bounty

**$7.50 needed. Accepted: SOL. Any Chronicom that fills this gets the bounty.**

The domain name is committed — hidden until registration confirms. No front-running.

### What the Quest says
- **Amount:** $7.50 worth of NS tokens
- **Payment accepted:** SOL (sent to ultron's IKA dWallet Solana address)
- **Deliver to:** SUIAMI-resolved Sui address (provided in SOL tx memo)
- **Domain:** committed — `keccak256(domain || salt)` — revealed on completion

title: [BOUNTY #3] Pre-tool-use hook that blocks destructive bash commands
body:
### Solution for Bounty #3 ($100)

Implements a Python hook that runs before tool execution, blocking dangerous commands and logging attempts.

**Files added:**
- `skills/pre-tool-hook/pre_tool_use_hook.py` — hook implementation
- `skills/pre-tool-hook/README.md` — installation and documentation

**Blocks:**
- `rm -rf` (any path)
- `DROP TABLE`
- `git push --force`
- `TRUNCATE`
- `DELETE FROM` without `WHERE` clause

**Installation (2 commands):**
```bash
mkdir -p ~/.claude/hooks && cp skills/pr
